page: Microsoft Azure HC44-16rs is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the HCS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8168 processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 352.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 22.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 717 GB volume, and the instance supports RDMA and Premium Storage. No GPUs are present on this configuration.

Benchmark results place the server in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is measured at 1414.09, while the multi-core score reaches 22570.21. Memory bandwidth testing shows a read-only small block cached speed of 1631.16 GB/sec and an uncached large block read speed of 164.07 GB/sec. Database operations via Passmark score at 10797.1, and Redis SET operations achieve 26.41 million operations per second.

Large-scale language model inference represents a significant performance bottleneck for this hardware configuration. While prompt processing for a 135M model reaches 1054.81 tokens per second, text generation for a 7B model drops to a weak 7.44 tokens per second, and 70B model prompt processing is poor at 4.52 tokens per second. Given the high memory capacity, dedicated CPU allocation, and RDMA capabilities, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and distributed scientific computing rather than heavy LLM text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure HC44-16rs is an x86_64 server in the HCS family, utilizing 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8168 processor running at 2.7 GHz. It features 352.0 GB of RAM, yielding 22.0 GB per core, alongside 717 GB of local storage and RDMA capability. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 22570.21 and a Passmark database score of 10797.1. However, LLM text generation is weak, with a 7B model achieving only 7.44 tokens per second. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and distributed computing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure HC44-16rs is an x86_64 server featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8168 cores, 352 GB of RAM, and 717 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.

tagline: A memory-dense x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores, RDMA capabilities, and substantial local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

352 GB of system memory providing 22 GB per core
717 GB of local storage with RDMA and Premium Storage support
Average database performance with 10,797.1 Passmark score
Weak performance for large language model text generation
Suited for memory-intensive applications and database workloads
